Array item field 'total_winnings': expected number, got string

findings.title: Button .btn-primary may lack cursor:pointer
findings.description: The .btn-primary button class does not explicitly set cursor:pointer. Depending on browser defaults, users may not see the pointer cursor when hovering.

findings.title: Button .btn-secondary may lack cursor:pointer
findings.description: The .btn-secondary button class does not explicitly set cursor:pointer. Depending on browser defaults, users may not see the pointer cursor when hovering.

findings.title: Button .btn-ghost may lack cursor:pointer
findings.description: The .btn-ghost button class does not explicitly set cursor:pointer. Depending on browser defaults, users may not see the pointer cursor when hovering.

findings.title: Button .btn-danger has no hover state
findings.description: The .btn-danger button class does not define a :hover style. Users cannot tell when they're hovering over the button, making it appear non-interactive.
reproductionSteps: Open any page with a .btn-danger button

findings.title: Button .btn-danger may lack cursor:pointer
findings.description: The .btn-danger button class does not explicitly set cursor:pointer. Depending on browser defaults, users may not see the pointer cursor when hovering.

findings.title: Button .btn-danger has no focus indicator
findings.description: The .btn-danger button class does not define a :focus style. Keyboard users cannot see which element has focus. WCAG 2.4.7 requires visible focus.
reproductionSteps: Open any page with a .btn-danger button
Tab to the button using keyboard
Notice no visual focus ring appears

findings.title: Button .btn-danger looks disabled due to low opacity
findings.description: The .btn-danger button has a background with only 22% opacity (rgba(127, 29, 29, 0.22)). This makes the button appear disabled or non-interactive when it is actually clickable.
reproductionSteps: Navigate to a page with a .btn-danger button (e.g., /bots page "Deactivate" button)
Click the button - it actually works

findings.title: low-contrast: .btn-danger
findings.description: Danger button has very low background opacity (22%) - may look disabled

findings.title: missing-hover-state: .btn-danger
findings.description: Danger button has no hover state defined - users cannot tell it's interactive

findings.title: missing-cursor-pointer: .btn-danger
findings.description: Danger button may not have cursor:pointer - needs explicit cursor style

findings.title: Typography: Limited font weight variety - typography feels flat...
findings.description:
Limited font weight variety - typography feels flat

**Suggestion:** Use at least 3 weights (400, 600, 700) for visual hierarchy

findings.title: Micro-interactions: Only 0 transitions found - interactions feel abrupt...
findings.description:
Only 0 transitions found - interactions feel abrupt

**Suggestion:** Add smooth transitions (150-300ms) to all interactive elements

**Competitor Reference:** Premium poker apps have buttery smooth 200ms transitions everywhere

findings.title: Interactivity: Only 6 hover states - many elements feel static...
findings.description:
Only 6 hover states - many elements feel static

**Suggestion:** Every clickable element needs a hover state. Users should feel elements respond

**Competitor Reference:** GGPoker has hover effects on every button, card, and link

findings.title: Accessibility: No :focus-visible styles - keyboard navigation feels broken...
findings.description:
No :focus-visible styles - keyboard navigation feels broken

**Suggestion:** Add :focus-visible styles for keyboard accessibility without affecting mouse users

findings.title: Animation: Only 0 keyframe animations - app feels static...
findings.description:
Only 0 keyframe animations - app feels static

**Suggestion:** Add animations for: loading states, card deals, chip movements, win celebrations

**Competitor Reference:** PokerStars has 20+ distinct animations for game events

findings.title: Animation: Limited use of CSS transforms - missing modern animation tec...
findings.description:
Limited use of CSS transforms - missing modern animation techniques

**Suggestion:** Use transform for scale, rotate, and translate animations (GPU accelerated)

findings.title: Dark Theme: Some text colors may have low contrast on dark backgrounds...
findings.description:
Some text colors may have low contrast on dark backgrounds

**Suggestion:** Ensure text has at least 4.5:1 contrast ratio. Use #e0e0e0+ for body text

findings.title: Overall Design Grade: B - Acceptable
findings.description:
The UI is functional and reasonably polished. Some areas could use more attention to compete with top-tier platforms.

**Breakdown:**
- Amateur-level issues: 0
- Mediocre areas: 3
- Minor suggestions: 4

**Competitors analyzed:** PokerStars, GGPoker, 888poker, PartyPoker, WPT Global

findings.title: INVARIANT VIOLATION: valid_card_format
findings.description: Invalid card format: A♠, A♣, 10♥, 3♣, 7♦, Q♥, 8♠, Q♦, Q♠

findings.description: Pot too small: current bets sum to 15000, but pot is only 0

findings.title: INVARIANT VIOLATION: chip_conservation
findings.description: Chip conservation violated: 198000 → 200000 (diff: 2000)

Only 7 hover states - many elements feel static

**Suggestion:** Every clickable element needs a hover state. Users should feel elements respond

**Competitor Reference:** GGPoker has hover effects on every button, card, and link

findings.title:
User Experience: Exposing internal ID in UI: {
    return playerNames[botId] ...
findings.description:
Exposing internal ID in UI: {
    return playerNames[botId] || botId.slice(0, 8)...

**Suggestion:** Use human-friendly identifiers (names, sequential numbers) instead of UUIDs. If an ID must be shown, use a friendly format like 'Table A3' or hide it entirely

**Competitor Reference:** PokerStars shows 'Table Madrid 24' not 'Table #a7f2b8c3'

findings.title: Overall Design Grade: C - Below Average
findings.description:
The UI has some professional elements but several areas feel unpolished. Players would notice the lack of refinement compared to competitors.

**Breakdown:**
- Amateur-level issues: 0
- Mediocre areas: 8
- Minor suggestions: 4

**Competitors analyzed:** PokerStars, GGPoker, 888poker, PartyPoker, WPT Global

findings.title: Micro-interactions: Only 5 transitions found - interactions feel abrupt...
findings.description:
Only 5 transitions found - interactions feel abrupt

**Suggestion:** Add smooth transitions (150-300ms) to all interactive elements

**Competitor Reference:** Premium poker apps have buttery smooth 200ms transitions everywhere

findings.title: Overall Design Grade: B+ - Good
findings.description:
The UI is well-designed with minor areas for improvement. Competitive with mid-tier poker platforms.

**Breakdown:**
- Amateur-level issues: 0
- Mediocre areas: 1
- Minor suggestions: 2

**Competitors analyzed:** PokerStars, GGPoker, 888poker, PartyPoker, WPT Global

findings.title: Overall Design Grade: A - Professional
findings.description:
The UI meets professional standards and could compete with established poker platforms.

**Breakdown:**
- Amateur-level issues: 0
- Mediocre areas: 0
- Minor suggestions: 1

**Competitors analyzed:** PokerStars, GGPoker, 888poker, PartyPoker, WPT Global
